change_comments.message:
I have not focussed on the groovy test at the moment.
But I would like to know what u think abt my comments ? 
Does it makes sense ? 

Especially on the Status and Details pojo object ? 

Others from the Team-2 can also look at this patch.
Please find my comments in line.

looks good to me.
This patch has grown enough I guess.

- Creating a generic concrete cloud event comment that I suggested can be done as part of separate story.
- Any other NB comments we can handle as part of a different patch.
Right, with the next upcoming task, will try to change mappers into a generic format. I will follow up NB comments on the next commit.
On file 16 will continue rest tomorrow

agreed with Lukes comment.
We can have have enum for SubscriptionStatus
I suggest to get that enum in another commit other than this 😊 to stop this commit groving more. What do you think?
You are faster than me Luke, while I was writing the last comment you submit this patchset 😊 so no need to answer that, since it is obvious now.

inline_comments.message:
NB: do you think we can make this generic and others can also reuse it.
eg : 
We name it CloudEventObjectMapper.
which has a map method which takes in cloudEvent and target class type and it returns the converted object of that class type ? 

Just like we have methods in ObjectMapper.
Then it would make sense to keep in the Utils package and can be used by other classes.
What do you think ?

NB: as we were talking yesterday , do we need to store the datastore-xpath-filter in the subscriptionCreated use case ??
If yes then the YANG model change is also needed.
If team agrees that it is needed , then we will create as part of separate task.
Not in this commit.
These status messages will LATER be read from the codes we will decide..right ??

does it need to be a Map of status and details ??
Y not a Custom class containing these 2 attributes for now ?
If it is a class then we can extend it.
What do you think ?

In my opinion these codes should be coming from a common place.
We will have codes corresponding to the messages.
Here both T1 and T2 need to agree.
We have a separate user story created to handle this.
can u paramterize the eventType here ??
add the parameter as eventType here.

again this message should come from a common place.
To be decided with the team and to be implemented separately.

would use target instead of CM Handle and reference NCMP:
Target has not been registered with NCMP
Can this be simplified? Theres a positive relying on a negative relying on a positive and is a bit confusing
I'd put this on its own line
Same again, try simplify positive based on negative based on positive
I'd extract out, too many method calls on one line
These codes were examples so we need to discuss what they should actually be as a team...

These variables dont need the From part, same for the rest
Can be original and new instead
would name either cmHandle or target
dont understand why these were changed apart from adding details, original test was to simulate a timeout because there are pending targets, this change has altered the scope of the test
These don't need to be on the table if its going to be 0 everytime

this can be condensed to 
objectUnderTest.toSubscriptionEventOutcome(subscriptionResponseEvent)
then'...'
thrown(DataValidationException)

no need to test details... but if you want you can do
DataValidationException exception = thrown(DataValidationException)
exception.details == "SubscriptionStatus list can not be null or empty"
Know you didn't write this but could you update to same exception catching format I left on other file thanks
Agree with that idea. However, I could suggest to handle it within another commit. If we try to do it in this commit, it would be much more bigger to trace and get it merged.

In my opinion having it like HashMap would be far more better than a class. Since HashMap has a constant search time 1 in terms of Big O Notation O(1) average. Also, in this way, there would no additional class and getter/setters will be required. If this could have been a class, it would be necessary to change the class for every single field new. I suggest to maintain HashMap other than a pojo class.
Lets make this change after getting this commit merged. Otherwise, it would be getting bigger 😊
These were meant to test a mid step that is persisting yang model into db. However I removed that db step from this test as suggested to keep test scope clear.

extract the common code to method and call the method.
do it in one line.

amm..this has to be the default message ??
or we make the constructor paramterize to accept the details..
just thinking..want to know your thinking around this.
I am fine either ways.

m not sure but coz of this change do we need to do something in liquibase??
or it will be handled with the new framework that Anjella worked on ??
The one with Spring events??
where does this status code coming ??
This is default message at the very beginning stage while subscription being forwarded, thus no need to have it in constructors, in my opinion.
It is being handled within new SubscriptionModelLoader now done by Anjealla. Since we also changed subscription.yang, the details field is persisted into the db.
